DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS EXCEPT FOR THE MODIFICATION AS TO DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR A BLANKET PROTECTIVE ORDER BARRING THE DEPOSITION OF THIRD-PARTY LAWLER BRANEMAN IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THAT "THE BURDEN, EXPENSE, OR INTRUSIVENESS OF [THE DEPOSITION] CLEARLY OUTWEIGHS THE LIKELIHOOD THAT THE INFORMATION SOUGHT WILL LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE." CCP 2017.020. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS ALSO DENIED. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS ARE TO PAY DISCOVERY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,600 WITHIN 20 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER FOR MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS UNDER CCP SEC. 2023.30.060(H), CCP SEC. 2025.420(H), AND CCP SEC. 128.5. NOTHING HEREIN SHALL BE CONSTRUED AS LIMITING THE RIGHT OF THE DEPONENT OR ANY PARTY ATTENDING THE DEPOSITION TO DEMAND THAT THE DEPOSITION BE SUSPENDED "TO ENABLE THAT PARTY OR DEPONENT TO MOVE FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER UNDER SECTION 2025.420 ON THE GROUND THAT THE EXAMINATION IS BEING CONDUCTED IN BAD FAITH OR IN A MANNER THAT UNREASONABLY ANNOYS, EMBARRASSES, OR OPPRESSES THAT DEPONENT OR PARTY." CCP 2025.470. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: ROGER MEAD; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: SUSAN D. YIP, CSR #5038, TEL: 415-469-7559, E-MAIL: SUSANDYIP@GMAIL.COM (302/JPT)

TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-21-2020 IN 220, AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED. COUNSEL TO SUBMIT THEIR RESPECTIVE PROPOSED ORDERS TO THE COURT BY MONDAY OCTOBER 5, 2020. PROCEEDINGS REPORTED BY DEBBIE FUQUA CSR# 12948 T: 415.810.7170 E:DFUQUACSR@GMAIL.COM; JUDGE: KATHLEEN KELLY; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY (D220).

TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-21-2020 IN 220, PLAINTIFFS AMENDED NOTICE OF REQUEST FOR ISSUANCE FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE AND ORDER PROHIBITING IMPROPER EMAILS TO THE COURT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE ORDER. THE COURT WILL IMPOSE SANCTIONS ON BOTH PLAINTIFFS' AND DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL FOR THEIR JOINT FAILURE TO ADHERE TO THE COURT'S ORDER OF JULY 13, 2020 TO MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH. THE COURT WILL ISSUE SANCTIONS ON COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $1500.00 EACH, PURSUANT TO CCP 177.5. SANCTIONS ARE TO BE PAID TO THE CLERK OF THE SUPERIOR COURT , COUNSTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. THE COURT WILL STAY THIS ORDER FOR 90 DAYS AND WILL PERMANENTLY STAY THE ORDER OF SANCTIONS IF COUNSEL PROVIDES THE COURT WITH PROOF OF COMPLETION OF AN APPROVED MCLE TRAINING PROGRAM FOCUSED ON PROPER PROFESSIONAL CONDUCT AND RESPONSIBILITY, PARTICULARLY IN THE CONTEXT OF COURTROOM COMPORTMENT AND ADHERING TO COURT ORDERS TO MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH BY DECEMBER 18, 2020 12:00PM. COUNSEL TO SUBMIT THEIR RESPECTIVE PROPOSED ORDERS TO THE COURT BY MONDAY OCTOBER 5, 2020. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO CONFIRM DEFENDANT CHRISTIE WESTS OWNERSHIP OF 1215 29TH AVENUE SAN FRANCISCO IS DENIED. THE COURT MADE ITS RULINGS REGARDING OWNERSHIP OF THE PROPERTY IN THE ORDERS ENTERED ON FEBRUARY 3, 2026. PLAINTIFFS HAD MOVED "FOR AN ORDER PERMITTING THE SALE OF A DWELLING OWNED BY THE JUDGMENT DEBTORS, CHRISTIE WEST AND TIMOTHY WEST." NO EVIDENCE OR ARGUMENT WAS PRESENTED BY THE PLAINTIFFS THAT THE PROPERTY (1215 29TH AVE, SF, CA) WAS OWNED BY BOTH CHRISTIE WEST AND TIMOTHY WEST AT THE SAME TIME. THUS, THE PROPERTY COULD BE OWNED BY MS. WEST OR MR. WEST NOTWITHSTANDING THE USE OF "AND" BY THE PLAINTIFF IN THE MOTION. THE ONLY UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE AS TO THE OWNERSHIP OF THE PROPERTY AS OF THE TIME OF THE REQUEST FOR SALE WAS (1) THE RECORDED TITLE REFLECTING MR. WEST'S OWNERSHIP; AND (2) MR. WEST'S ADMISSION OF OWNERSHIP. ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFFS EXPRESSLY STATED THAT AS PLAINTIFFS HAVE A JUDGMENT AGAINST MR. WEST, THE SALE IS PROPER AS TO HIM. IN OTHER WORDS, BY SEEKING A SALE OF THE PROPERTY TO SATISFY MR. WEST'S DEBT, PLAINTIFFS CONCEDED HIS OWNERSHIP; OTHERWISE A REQUEST TO SELL PROPERTY THAT DOES NOT BELONG TO THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR TO SATISFY A DEBT WOULD BE FRIVOLOUS AND NOT SUPPORTED BY ANY LAW CITED IN THE APPLICATION FOR SALE OF DWELLING. THEREFORE, HAVING PLAINTIFFS' CONCESSION OF OWNERSHIP AS REFLECTED BY THE FILING OF THE APPLICATION FOR SALE OF DWELLING TO SATISFY MR. WEST'S DEBT, RECORDED TITLE, AND ADMISSION OF CURRENT OWNERSHIP BY MR. WEST, THE COURT ISSUED ITS FEBRUARY 3, 2026 ORDERS. (PLEASE SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion To Confirm Defendant Christie Wests Ownership Of 1215 29th Avenue San Francisco
Off Calendar Apr-03-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Hooshmand; 415-523-3400; Hooshmand Law Group; counsel appearing for plaintiff.
Frank Kim; 415-567-5700; National Legal Associates; counsel for defendant Timothy West appearing via Zoom.
Christie West;415-694-0430 defendant appearing in pro per.
Reporter: Susan Yip; CSR# 5038; 415-412-1678; susandyip@gmail.com

Mr. Hooshmand contests the courts tentative ruling. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBINS Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion To Confirm Defendant Christie Wests Ownership Of 1215 29th Avenue San Francisco is DENIED. The Court made its rulings regarding ownership of the property in the orders entered on February 3, 2026. Plaintiffs had moved "for an order permitting the sale of a dwelling owned by the judgment debtors, Christie West and Timothy West." No evidence or argument was presented by the Plaintiffs that the property (1215 29th Ave, SF, CA) was owned by both Christie West and Timothy West at the same time. Thus, the property could be owned by Ms. West or Mr. West notwithstanding the use of "and" by the Plaintiff in the motion. The only undisputed evidence as to the ownership of the Property as of the time of the request for sale was (1) the recorded title reflecting Mr. West's ownership; and (2) Mr. West's admission of ownership. Additionally, Plaintiffs expressly stated that as Plaintiffs have a judgment against Mr. West, the sale is proper as to him. In other words, by seeking a sale of the property to satisfy Mr. West's debt, Plaintiffs conceded his ownership; otherwise a request to sell property that does not belong to the judgment debtor to satisfy a debt would be frivolous and not supported by any law cited in the Application for Sale of Dwelling. Therefore, having Plaintiffs' concession of ownership as reflected by the filing of the Application for Sale of Dwelling to satisfy Mr. West's debt, recorded title, and admission of current ownership by Mr. West, the Court issued its February 3, 2026 orders.
To the extent Plaintiff may be seeking a reconsideration of the Court's February 3, 2026 orders, the Court makes no determination of the validity of such motion as it is not properly before the Court.
To the extent Plaintiff relies on the language in the February 4, 2020 judgement for res judicata/collateral estoppel re: Ms. West's ownership, Plaintiffs failed to establish its applicability. (Language purportedly adjudicating Christie West's ownership per supplemental declaration filed on March 27, 2026: "The jury awarded punitive damages in favor of Plaintiff Nina Robin and as against Defendant Christie West in the amount of $15,000. The jury awarded punitive damages in favor of Plaintiff Nina Robin and as against Defendant Timothy West in the amount of $5,000.")
As to the March 27, 2026 Supplemental Hooshmand Declaration, it is stricken (except for paragraphs 1, 2 and the first sentence of paragraph 3) as unauthorized.
March 31, 2026 Declaration of Christie West is stricken. The filing was not authorized.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Application And Application For Sale Of Real Property Located At 1215 29th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94122
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Hooshmand for plaintiffs Jennifer Sarkany, et al.
Frank Kim and Joseph O'Neil for defendant Timothy West, who was also present.
Christie West, defendant in pro per.

The matter was called for hearing on:
1) Plaintiff's Application for Sale of Real Property Located at 1215 29th Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94122;
2) Plaintiffs' Motion to Find Liability as to Defendant Timothy West; and
3) Plaintiffs' Notice of Hearing on Right to Homestead Exemption.

Mr. Hooshmand stated that all moving papers, including an update and summary regarding the motion to sell real property, had been served on all parties. Christie West stated she had not been served. Based on the proofs of service on file, the Court found service sufficient. Mr. Kim and Mr. O'Neil confirmed receipt and review of all recent filings.

The Court heard argument regarding ownership of the subject real property. Mr. Hooshmand argued that prior jury findings and the filings submitted established that Christie West was the owner of the property. Mr. Kim and Mr. O'Neil, joined by Christie West, argued that ownership had not been adjudicated for declaratory purposes and that prior jury findings were limited to punitive damages.

After hearing argument and reviewing the filings submitted prior to the hearing, the Court found, based on the record before it, that Christie West was the owner of the subject property for purposes of the pending motions.

The Court heard further argument concerning homestead exemptions. Mr. Hooshmand argued that Timothy West's homestead claim was untimely and unsupported and that Christie West was precluded from asserting a homestead exemption because she had already filed an exemption for a property in Texas. Mr. Kim argued that homestead issues depended on ownership and residency and required further consideration.

With respect to plaintiffs' motion seeking a finding of liability as to defendant Timothy West, Mr. Hooshmand argued that liability had been established based on prior jury findings. Mr. O'Neil opposed the motion as procedurally improper and barred by prior rulings, and requested sanctions. Mr. Kim joined in opposition. The Court heard argument from all parties.

After hearing argument, no rulings were issued at the hearing. The Court took the matters under submission and directed counsel to comply with CRC Rule 3.1312(b) and submit proposed orders and supporting materials consistent with the Court's findings.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Judith C. DeAlba, CSR #5709, judy@dealbareportingservice.com, 650-571-1473 (D501)

Notice Of Application And Application For Sale Of Real Property Located At 1215 29th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94122
Off Calendar Dec-18-2025 Continued To Jan-02-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Hooshmand for plaintiffs Jennifer Sarkany, et al.
Frank Kim and Joseph O'Neil for defendant Timothy West, who was also present.
Christie West, defendant in pro per.

The matter was called for hearing on:
1) Plaintiff's Application for Sale of Real Property Located at 1215 29th Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94122;
2) Plaintiffs' Motion to Find Liability as to Defendant Timothy West; and
3) Plaintiffs' Notice of Hearing on Right to Homestead Exemption.

Mr. Hooshmand raised a procedural objection to Mr. O'Neil's representation of Timothy West, following his prior withdrawl as counsel. Mr. O'Neil stated he appeared at Timothy West's request, and Timothy West confirmed on the record that Mr. O'Neil was representing him.

Mr. O'Neill referenced a request for judicial notice, including an excerpt from a September 28, 2023 tentative ruling by Judge Kelly regarding damages, and argued the motion improperly sought to relitigate issues previously determined. Mr. Hooshmand objected to the request for judicial notice. Mr. Kim reported that he had located a loan preapproval letter indicating Timothy West was preapproved for a $600,000 loan, and requested time to complete the loan process. Mr. Hooshmand opposed further delay, objected to late-filed, unauthenticated documents, and argued defendants had a history of last-minute filings and had not complied with prior court-ordered updates.

The Court noted that CM-181 had been filed, and continued all matters to January 2, 2026, to allow time for the loan process and for the sale-related issues to be addressed. The Court ordered that any additional filings be made no later than close of business on December 23, 2025, and made a good-cause finding to continue with waiver of notice. The Court indicated the issues, including the homestead notice, would be addressed on January 2, 2026.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Susan Yip, CSR #5038, susandyip@gmail.com, 415-412-1678 (D501)

Notice Of Application And Application For Sale Of Real Property Located At 1215 29th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94122
Off Calendar Sep-17-2025 Continued To Oct-13-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Hooshmand for plaintiff Jennifer Sarkany.
Christie West, defendant in Pro Per.
Frank Kim for defendant Timothy West (via Zoom).

The matter came on for hearing on 1) Plaintiff's Motion Finding Defendants in Contempt for Violating Court Orders, Request for Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants Christie West and Timothy West and Attorney Frank Kim; 2) Plaintiff's Application for Sale of Real Property; 3) Plaintiff's Motion to Find Liability as to Defendant Timothy West; 4) Plaintiffs' Notice of Hearing on Right to Homestead Exemption; and 5) Order to Show Cause re: Sale of Dwelling.

Discussion was held regarding the bankruptcy action currently pending as to defendant Timothy West. Mr. Hooshmand argued that under CCP 704.770 and 704.800, failure to make a good cause finding would bar plaintiff from re-filing for one year. He asked the Court to find good cause in light of the bankruptcy stay. Mr. Kim argued that the notice of bankruptcy filed September 10th deprived the Court of jurisdiction, and proceedings should have stopped as of that date.

Regarding the motion for contempt against defendants, Mr. Hooshmand argued that Ms. West violated court orders by failing to provide responses to discovery (special interrogatories, production of documents, and subpoenaed OEX documents). Ms. West claimed she delivered responses, but they were not acknowledged. The Court noted materials Ms. West brought to the hearing referenced a different case number from a 2024 case, and were not the discovery at issue.

After hearing oral argument, the Court ruled as follows:

Plaintiff shall lodge discovery requests with the Court by noon September 18, 2025. Defendant Christie West shall pick up the discovery requests from the Court not later than September 23, 2025. Ms. West shall deliver responses to discovery to the Court by October 13, 2025. The Court found good cause in light of the bankruptcy stay, and continued the underlying matters to October 13, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 501. Proceedings as to Timothy West remain subject to the bankruptcy stay. Mr. Kim was ordered to notify the Court if Timothy West's bankruptcy is resolved earlier than October 13th.

Mr. Hooshmand was instructed to give notice and submit a proposed order consistent with the Court's rulings.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Judith DeAlba, CSR #5709, judy@dealbareportingservice.com, 650-571-1473 (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Contempt And To Compel Document Production From Defendant Christie West; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Christie West
Off Calendar Jul-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. Mark Hooshmand, Esq. (via Zoom videoconference) of Hooshmand Law Group (tel: 415-4233400) for the Plaintiffs.
Defendant Christie West, in pro per present.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, AND NINA ROBINS' Motion For Contempt And To Compel Document Production From Defendant Christie West; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Christie West.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs Jennifer Sarkany et al. move for an order compelling document production by defendant Christie West, for monetary sanctions, and for an order of contempt. The motion is unopposed. The court grants the motion in part and denies it in part.

Sarkany shows that West was previously ordered to produce documents pursuant to her request for production. The transcript of hearing from the initial debtor's examination (Exhibit E to Hooshmand Dec.) shows that West redacted some of the documents in part. The Hooshmand Declaration further demonstrates that West has not produced all documents responsive to the deposition subpoena personally served on her. (See Hooshmand Dec. paras. 10, 12 & Ex. C.)

The court orders West to produce unredacted documents responsive to the deposition subpoena attached as Exhibit C to the Hooshmand Declaration by August 1, 2025, a date in advance of the continued examination. The court finds that West has misused the discovery process. The court grants the motion for sanctions and orders West to pay $3525 to Plaintiffs by August 1, 2025.

Sarkany further seeks a contempt order. Contempt (which can subject the contemnor to jail or to other penalties such as fines that accrue daily) is a last resort. The court denies the request but cautions West that further violations of court orders may subject her to an order to show cause why she should not be subject to contempt sanctions.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion For A Protective Order Concerning Deposition Of Lawler Braneman And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. MARK HOOSHMAND, ESQ. of Hooshmand Law Group (Tel: 415-318-5709) appearing for the plaintiffs;
Atty. JOSEPH D. O'NEIL, ESQ. of Tarkington, O'Neill, Barrack & Chong (Tel: 415-777-5501) appearing for defendants.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, AND NINA ROBINS' Motion For A Protective Order Concerning Deposition Of Lawler Braneman And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Counsel for both sides stipulate to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows except for the modification as to defendants' request for sanctions: Plaintiffs' motion for a blanket protective order barring the deposition of third-party Lawler Braneman is denied. Plaintiffs have not established that "the burden, expense, or intrusiveness of [the deposition] clearly outweighs the likelihood that the information sought will lead to the discovery of admissible evidence." CCP 2017.020. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is also denied.

Defendants' request for sanctions is granted. Plaintiffs are to pay discovery sanctions in the amount of $2,600 within 20 days of service of Notice of Entry of this Order for misuse of the discovery process under CCP Sec. 2023.30.060(h), CCP Sec. 2025.420(h), and CCP Sec. 128.5.

Nothing herein shall be construed as limiting the right of the deponent or any party attending the deposition to demand that the deposition be suspended "to enable that party or deponent to move for a protective order under Section 2025.420 on the ground that the examination is being conducted in bad faith or in a manner that unreasonably annoys, embarrasses, or oppresses that deponent or party." CCP 2025.470.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Susan D. Yip, CSR #5038, Tel: 415-469-7559, e-mail: susandyip@gmail.com (302/JPT)
